assessment_items.question_content:
**The painting is based on a [[☃ dropdown 1]], but rooted in the common practice among slave ship captains to throw sick slaves overboard in order to collect insurance money.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/07ac7abaec16c163eeb9364d2637510d.png)
hints: **The painting is based on a poem, but rooted in the common practice among slave ship captains to throw sick slaves overboard in order to collect insurance money.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The  [[☃ dropdown 1]] suggests the divine power of nature to punish the slave traders, but it remains indifferent to the struggle of the drowning slaves.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/07ac7abaec16c163eeb9364d2637510d.png)
hints: **The looming typhoon suggests the divine power of nature to punish the slave traders, but it remains indifferent to the struggle of the drowning slaves.**

assessment_items.question_content: **First owned by John Ruskin, *Slave Ship* was sold to an American [[☃ dropdown 1]], reminding us that in 1840 slavery was still a charged political issue in the United States.**
hints: **First owned by John Ruskin, *Slave Ship* was sold to an American abolitionist reminding us that in 1840 slavery was still a charged political issue in the United States.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**At first glance, the viewer sees only the beauty of Turner's sensual use of paint and color before the horror of the subject becomes apparent.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/07ac7abaec16c163eeb9364d2637510d.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **Turner's use of color is visual focus of this painting, even more than its political message.**
